humanSummary.text: Audit found no P0/P1 blockers. The dashboard decision read model distinguishes project-level, locale-branch, and runtime validation decision counts and has DB/API/MSW/dashboard tests. Stricter API kind invariants and DB row mapping fail-closed behavior are recorded as follow-ups.

findings.title: Decision read-model kind invariant validation
findings.description: The response validator checks shape and count consistency, but does not enforce kind-specific invariants such as `project_finding` requiring `localeBranchId === null` or `runtime_validation` requiring `runtimeRunId !== null`.

evidence.detail: Dashboard audit found current schema validation sufficient for acceptance but weaker than the semantic kind model.

findings.impact: Future API or repository drift could produce internally contradictory decision records while still satisfying structural validation.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Dashboard decision read-model validation enforces kind-specific nullable field invariants.
API tests reject contradictory project_finding, locale_branch_finding, and runtime_validation examples.

proposedDagNode.title: Dashboard decision kind invariant validation
proposedDagNode.summary: Strengthen dashboard decision read-model schema validation with kind-specific nullable field invariants.
deliverables: Decision read-model validator enforcing project, locale-branch, and runtime-validation invariants
API schema tests for contradictory decision records
acceptanceCriteria: `project_finding` records require null localeBranchId and runtimeRunId.
`runtime_validation` records require runtimeRunId and cannot be counted as branch findings.

verification.value: pnpm exec vitest run apps/itotori/test/api-handlers.test.ts apps/itotori/test/dashboard.test.ts

auditFocus: Dashboard decision record kind contradicting nullable fields

findings.title: Decision row mapping fail-closed coercion
findings.description: The DB row mapper uses string coercion for finding IDs. A SQL alias drift could become the literal string `undefined` instead of failing close to the source.

evidence.detail: Dashboard audit identified `String(row.finding_id)` as a non-blocking fail-closed weakness in row mapping.

findings.impact: A future query alias regression could produce misleading IDs rather than a direct validation failure.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Decision row mapping uses explicit nullable/string guards instead of blind `String(...)` coercion.
Repository tests fail when required decision row aliases are missing or null.

proposedDagNode.title: Review queue row mapping fail-closed guards
proposedDagNode.summary: Replace silent string coercion in dashboard decision row mapping with explicit fail-closed guards and tests.
deliverables: Guarded dashboard decision row mapper in packages/itotori-db
Repository tests for missing or null required decision row aliases
acceptanceCriteria: Missing finding_id aliases fail with semantic repository diagnostics or test-visible errors.
No dashboard decision ID can become the literal string `undefined`.
auditFocus: Silent string coercion hiding SQL alias drift
